fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: New Hampshire CNA License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your Certification (2026)
seo.description: Already certified as a CNA? Here's whether your certification transfers to New Hampshire - endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: CNA license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer CNA license to new hampshire, new hampshire CNA endorsement application

license_types.title: Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a nursing assistant in New Hampshire.

steps.title: Complete an Approved Training Program
steps.description: Successfully complete a Nursing Assistant training program approved by the New Hampshire Board of Nursing.

steps.title: Pass the NNAAP Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Nurse Aide Assessment Program (NNAAP) written and skills examinations within 24 months of your application date.

steps.title: Create an OPLC Account
steps.description: Navigate to the NH Online Licensing System portal (oplc.nh.gov/online/) and register for a new account by clicking 'Register for an Account' and following the prompts.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log into your OPLC account, select 'Apply for a New License,' and complete the Certified Nursing Assistant application form. Ensure all required documentation is uploaded as specified in the application.

steps.title: Undergo Background Check
steps.description: Complete the required criminal record background check as part of the application process. Instructions will be provided within the NH Online Licensing System.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the non-refundable initial application fee of $50.00 through the NH Online Licensing System using a credit or debit card.

documents.detail: Certificate of completion from your approved CNA training program.
Official verification of passing your state's nurse aide competency evaluation.

provisional_text: Some states allow CNAs to work under supervision for a limited period while their endorsement application is being processed.
reciprocity_text: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

faqs.question: How much does New Hampshire CNA endorsement cost?
faqs.answer: The endorsement application fee is $63. Additional costs may include background check fees.

faqs.question: Is a background check required?
faqs.answer: Yes, a fingerprint-based background check is required in New Hampshire.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my CNA certification to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while waiting?
faqs.answer: Check with the state board about temporary practice permits while your endorsement is being processed.

reciprocity.notes: CNAs certified in another state can often apply via endorsement (reciprocity) by providing registry verification from their home state.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become a LNA in NH.

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: Transfer an active out-of-state CNA to New Hampshire.

hero.title: Become a LNA in New Hampshire
hero.dek: To apply for a Certified Nursing Assistant (CNA) license in New Hampshire, you must create an account and submit your application through the NH Online Licensing System.

seo.title: How to Become a LNA in New Hampshire
seo.description: Step-by-step guide to New Hampshire LNA certification.

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the NH Online Licensing System portal. Click 'Register for an Account,' provide your personal details, create a user ID and password, and then log in to your account. Once logged in, select 'Apply for a New License' to start the Certified Nursing Assistant application.
